title: Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Scoring
text: sci_config_default() returns pillar weights decision-band thresholds: default weights : quality 35%, trace 25%, risk 25%, usability 15%.
code: cfg <- sci_config_default() cfg$pillar_weights #>   quality     trace      risk usability  #>      0.35      0.25      0.25      0.15 cfg$bands #> $ready #> [1]  85 100 #>  #> $minor_gaps #> [1] 70 84 #>  #> $conditional #> [1] 50 69 #>  #> $high_risk #> [1]  0 49

title: Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Scoring
code: ev <- data.frame(   run_id           = "run-001",   study_id         = "STUDY01",   asset_type       = "dataset",   asset_id         = rep(c("ADSL", "ADAE"), each = 6),   source_name      = "r4subcore",   source_version   = "0.1.2",   indicator_id     = paste0("X-00", 1:12),   indicator_name   = paste0("Indicator ", 1:12),   indicator_domain = rep(c("quality", "trace", "risk", "usability",                            "quality", "trace"), 2),   severity         = "info",   result           = c("pass", "pass", "pass", "pass", "pass", "pass",                        "warn", "pass", "fail", "pass", "pass", "warn"),   metric_value     = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 0.8, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0.9),   metric_unit      = "score",   message          = paste0("Indicator ", 1:12, " result"),   location         = rep(c("ADSL", "ADAE"), each = 6),   evidence_payload = "{}",   created_at       = Sys.time(),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )

title: Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Scoring
text: compute_pillar_scores() aggregates mean metric value per domain:
code: ps <- compute_pillar_scores(ev) ps #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   pillar    pillar_score n_indicators weight #>   <chr>            <dbl>        <int>  <dbl> #> 1 quality          0.875            4   0.35 #> 2 trace            0.875            4   0.25 #> 3 risk             0.5              2   0.25 #> 4 usability        1                2   0.15

title: Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Scoring
code: result <- compute_sci(ps) result$SCI #> [1] 80 result$band #> [1] "minor_gaps" print(result) #> ℹ Submission Confidence Index: 80 #> ℹ Decision Band: "minor_gaps" #> ℹ   quality: 87.5 (weight: 0.35) #> ℹ   trace: 87.5 (weight: 0.25) #> ℹ   risk: 50 (weight: 0.25) #> ℹ   usability: 100 (weight: 0.15)

title: Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Scoring
text: sci_explain() returns breakdown showing pillar’s contribution final SCI score loss:
code: expl <- sci_explain(ev) expl$pillar_contributions #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   pillar    pillar_score n_indicators weight contribution  loss #>   <chr>            <dbl>        <int>  <dbl>        <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 quality          0.875            4   0.35         30.6   4.4 #> 2 trace            0.875            4   0.25         21.9   3.1 #> 3 risk             0.5              2   0.25         12.5  12.5 #> 4 usability        1                2   0.15         15     0

title: Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Scoring
text: sci_sensitivity_analysis() evaluates SCI across grid alternative pillar weights, useful understanding robust score weighting choices:
code: grid <- data.frame(   quality    = c(0.35, 0.50, 0.25),   trace      = c(0.25, 0.20, 0.30),   risk       = c(0.25, 0.20, 0.25),   usability  = c(0.15, 0.10, 0.20) )  sa <- sci_sensitivity_analysis(ev, weight_grid = grid) sa #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   scenario quality trace  risk usability   SCI band       #>      <int>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> <chr>      #> 1        1    0.35  0.25  0.25      0.15  80   minor_gaps #> 2        2    0.5   0.2   0.2       0.1   81.2 minor_gaps #> 3        3    0.25  0.3   0.25      0.2   80.6 minor_gaps

title: Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Scoring
text: Pass custom config favour particular pillars:
code: cfg_custom <- sci_config_default(   pillar_weights = c(quality = 0.50, trace = 0.20,                      risk = 0.20, usability = 0.10) ) ps2   <- compute_pillar_scores(ev, config = cfg_custom) sci2  <- compute_sci(ps2, config = cfg_custom) sci2$SCI #> [1] 81.2 sci2$band #> [1] "minor_gaps"

title: r4subscore — Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Engine
text: r4subscore scoring calibration engine R4SUB ecosystem. converts standardized evidence (r4subcore companion packages) Submission Confidence Index (SCI) — single 0–100 score decision bands, explainability tables, sensitivity analysis. ready regulatory submission — confident ?

title: r4subscore — Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Engine
text: Development version:
code: install.packages("r4subscore") pak::pak(c("R4SUB/r4subcore", "R4SUB/r4subscore"))

title: r4subscore — Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Engine
code: library(r4subcore) library(r4subscore)  pillar_scores <- compute_pillar_scores(ev) sci           <- compute_sci(pillar_scores)  sci$SCI   # 0–100 sci$band  # "ready", "minor_gaps", "conditional", or "high_risk"

title: r4subscore — Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Engine
text: evidence row gets weighted score: result_score × (1 − severity_weight) Indicator scores = mean weighted score per indicator Pillar scores = mean indicator score per domain (quality, trace, risk, usability) SCI = weighted sum pillar scores × 100

title: r4subscore — Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Engine
code: library(r4subprofile) library(r4subscore)  prof <- submission_profile("FDA", "NDA") cfg  <- profile_sci_config(prof)  pillar_scores <- compute_pillar_scores(ev, config = cfg) sci           <- compute_sci(pillar_scores, config = cfg)

title: r4subscore — Submission Confidence Index (SCI) Engine

title: Classify SCI Value into Decision Band — classify_band
code: classify_band(sci_value, bands = sci_config_default()$bands)

title: Classify SCI Value into Decision Band — classify_band
text: sci_value Numeric SCI score (0–100). bands Named list band boundaries sci_config_default().

title: Classify SCI Value into Decision Band — classify_band
text: Character band name.

title: Classify SCI Value into Decision Band — classify_band
code: classify_band(92) #> [1] "ready" classify_band(55) #> [1] "conditional"

title: Compute Indicator-Level Scores — compute_indicator_scores
text: Converts indicator evidence table numeric score (0–1) using severity-weighted result scoring.

title: Compute Indicator-Level Scores — compute_indicator_scores
code: compute_indicator_scores(evidence)

title: Compute Indicator-Level Scores — compute_indicator_scores
text: evidence validated evidence data.frame (r4subcore).

title: Compute Indicator-Level Scores — compute_indicator_scores
text: tibble columns: indicator_id, indicator_name, indicator_domain, n_evidence, indicator_score.

title: Compute Indicator-Level Scores — compute_indicator_scores
text: evidence row: result_score = r4subcore::result_to_score(result) (pass=1, warn=0.5, fail=0) severity_weight = r4subcore::severity_to_weight(severity) (info=0, ..., critical=1) weighted_score = result_score * (1 - severity_weight) Rows grouped indicator_id indicator_domain, indicator score mean weighted_score within group.

title: Compute Indicator-Level Scores — compute_indicator_scores
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ scores <- compute_indicator_scores(evidence) scores } # }

title: Compute Pillar Scores — compute_pillar_scores
text: Aggregates indicator scores pillar-level scores (one per domain). pillar score mean indicator scores.

title: Compute Pillar Scores — compute_pillar_scores
code: compute_pillar_scores(evidence, config = sci_config_default())

title: Compute Pillar Scores — compute_pillar_scores
text: evidence validated evidence data.frame. config sci_config sci_config_default().

title: Compute Pillar Scores — compute_pillar_scores
text: tibble columns: pillar, pillar_score, n_indicators, weight.

title: Compute Submission Confidence Index (SCI) — compute_sci
text: Computes SCI pillar scores weighted sum scaled 0–100, decision band classification.

title: Compute Submission Confidence Index (SCI) — compute_sci
code: compute_sci(pillar_scores, config = sci_config_default())

title: Compute Submission Confidence Index (SCI) — compute_sci
text: pillar_scores tibble compute_pillar_scores() columns pillar, pillar_score, weight. config sci_config sci_config_default().

title: Compute Submission Confidence Index (SCI) — compute_sci
text: list class "sci_result" : SCI: numeric 0–100 band: character band classification pillar_scores: input pillar scores tibble weights_used: named numeric vector effective weights

title: Compute Submission Confidence Index (SCI) — compute_sci
text: SCI computed : SCI = round(sum(pillar_score * weight) * 100, 1) Pillars NA scores excluded numerator weight normalization denominator.

title: Compute Submission Confidence Index (SCI) — compute_sci
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ps <- compute_pillar_scores(evidence) result <- compute_sci(ps) result$SCI result$band } # }

what: r4subscore: Submission Confidence Index Engine for Clinical Submission Readiness — r4subscore-package
title: r4subscore: Submission Confidence Index Engine for Clinical Submission Readiness — r4subscore-package
text: Converts standardized R4SUB evidence indicator scores, pillar scores, Submission Confidence Index (SCI). Provides sensitivity analysis, explainability tables, decision band classification answer question: ready regulatory submission?

title: r4subscore: Submission Confidence Index Engine for Clinical Submission Readiness — r4subscore-package
text: Maintainer: Pawan Rama Mali prm@outlook.

title: Default SCI Configuration — sci_config_default
text: Returns configuration list default pillar weights, decision bands, scoring parameters Submission Confidence Index.

title: Default SCI Configuration — sci_config_default
code: sci_config_default(   pillar_weights = c(quality = 0.35, trace = 0.25, risk = 0.25, usability = 0.15),   bands = list(ready = c(85, 100), minor_gaps = c(70, 84), conditional = c(50, 69),     high_risk = c(0, 49)) )

title: Default SCI Configuration — sci_config_default
text: pillar_weights Named numeric vector weights pillar. Must sum 1. Names must subset "quality", "trace", "risk", "usability". bands Named list numeric length-2 vectors defining SCI band boundaries c(lower, upper). Evaluated order; first match wins.

title: Default SCI Configuration — sci_config_default
text: list class "sci_config" elements: pillar_weights, bands.

title: Default SCI Configuration — sci_config_default
code: cfg <- sci_config_default() cfg$pillar_weights #>   quality     trace      risk usability  #>      0.35      0.25      0.25      0.15  cfg$bands #> $ready #> [1]  85 100 #>  #> $minor_gaps #> [1] 70 84 #>  #> $conditional #> [1] 50 69 #>  #> $high_risk #> [1]  0 49 #>   # Custom weights (must sum to 1) sci_config_default(   pillar_weights = c(quality = 0.40, trace = 0.20, risk = 0.30, usability = 0.10) ) #> $pillar_weights #>   quality     trace      risk usability  #>       0.4       0.2       0.3       0.1  #>  #> $bands #> $bands$ready #> [1]  85 100 #>  #> $bands$minor_gaps #> [1] 70 84 #>  #> $bands$conditional #> [1] 50 69 #>  #> $bands$high_risk #> [1]  0 49 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "sci_config"

title: Explain SCI Contributors — sci_explain
text: Identifies indicators contribute SCI loss provides breakdown pillar contributions.

title: Explain SCI Contributors — sci_explain
code: sci_explain(evidence, config = sci_config_default())

title: Explain SCI Contributors — sci_explain
text: evidence validated evidence data.frame. config sci_config sci_config_default().

title: Explain SCI Contributors — sci_explain
text: list : indicator_contributions: tibble per-indicator loss contributions pillar_contributions: tibble per-pillar contributions SCI

title: Explain SCI Contributors — sci_explain
text: indicator, contribution SCI loss : loss = pillar_weight * (1 - indicator_score) / n_indicators_in_pillar gives sense much indicator drags SCI . Results sorted loss descending (worst contributors first).

title: Explain SCI Contributors — sci_explain
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ expl <- sci_explain(evidence) expl$indicator_contributions expl$pillar_contributions } # }

title: SCI Sensitivity Analysis — sci_sensitivity_analysis
text: Evaluates stability Submission Confidence Index alternative pillar weight scenarios.

title: SCI Sensitivity Analysis — sci_sensitivity_analysis
code: sci_sensitivity_analysis(evidence, weight_grid)

title: SCI Sensitivity Analysis — sci_sensitivity_analysis
text: evidence validated evidence data.frame. weight_grid data.frame row weight scenario. Column names must match pillar names (quality, trace, risk, usability). row must sum 1.

title: SCI Sensitivity Analysis — sci_sensitivity_analysis
text: tibble one row per scenario, containing: scenario (row number), weight columns, SCI, band.

title: SCI Sensitivity Analysis — sci_sensitivity_analysis
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ grid <- data.frame(   quality   = c(0.4, 0.3, 0.25),   trace     = c(0.2, 0.3, 0.25),   risk      = c(0.3, 0.2, 0.25),   usability = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.25) ) sci_sensitivity_analysis(evidence, grid) } # }
